case_name: JAH v Burne & Ors [2018] EWHC 3461 (QB) (14 December 2018)

evidence_index.text: In July 2012, the blood supply to the Claimant's left arm became compromised by a thrombo-embolus which led to a below-elbow amputation on 19 July 2012, and then an above-elbow amputation on 24 July 2012.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The First Defendant is a General Practitioner who, on 8 May 2012, was working as a locum at Ryalls Park Surgery in Yeovil where the Claimant was registered as a patient.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Second Defendant is now retired but was, in May, a partner at the Ryalls Park Medical Centre and she practised as a GP for 35 years.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Third Defendant, also a GP at the practice, saw the Claimant on 11 June 2012.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Fourth Defendant is the NHS Trust responsible for the staff and treatment at Yeovil District Hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Professor Beard, the Defendants' expert in vascular surgery, told me that this might have been the first sign of the micro-embolic process starting.
evidence_index.speaker: Professor Beard

evidence_index.text: Dr Stevens was an excellent expert witness whose evidence I preferred to that of Mr Richmond, both generally and in relation to the issue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I reject all the allegations of breach of duty arising out of the attendance on 6 May 2012 and I find that Nurse Foran acted reasonably, professionally and responsibly.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: For the above reasons, I find that causation is established against the Third Defendant, Dr Jackson in relation to the arm alone, thus entitling the Claimant to damages in the agreed sum of £150,000.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: For the reasons stated in this judgment, the claim is dismissed against the First, Second and Fourth Defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain in calves and left foot
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with a history of bilateral posterior calf pain when walking and left foot pain.

focused_exam.lower_extremities: Calf circumferences equal, no skin changes apart from thread veins, tender inferior calf muscle

requestables.name: Vascular Surgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with a vascular surgeon to assess the need for anticoagulation and further vascular intervention.

reveal.result_summary: Consultation not performed; urgent referral was indicated.
reveal.clinical_significance: Could have led to timely anticoagulation and prevention of upper limb ischaemia.
reveal.decision_impact: Referral would have prompted anticoagulation, potentially preventing arm amputation.

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with foot and leg pain.

timeline_phases.name: Workup and Missed Referral
timeline_phases.description: Assessment of the patient's condition without appropriate referral.

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with foot pain and preserved pedal pulses.
decision_points.prompt: Should an urgent vascular referral be made?

options.clinical_reasoning: Referral was necessary to assess for thrombo-embolic cause and initiate anticoagulation.

options.description: Do not make a vascular referral
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant did not refer, missing the opportunity for timely intervention.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not make a vascular referral
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: No indication for referral was perceived due to preserved pedal pulses.

expected_action_court.description: Urgent vascular referral should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated referral was necessary to prevent further complications.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to refer led to missed opportunity for anticoagulation.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral would have allowed for timely intervention.

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed upper limb ischaemia leading to amputation of the left arm.
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of left arm, significant impact on quality of life.

key_findings: Breach of duty by Dr Jackson for not making an urgent vascular referral.
Causation established for the arm amputation due to lack of anticoagulation.

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to make an urgent vascular referral
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Referral would have led to anticoagulation, preventing arm ischaemia.

allegations.allegation: Failure to make an urgent vascular referral

court_findings.finding: Dr Jackson breached duty of care
